2603.19733.title: PoC: Performance-oriented Context Compression for Large Language Models via Performance Prediction
2603.19733.paper_summary: While context compression can mitigate the growing inference costs of Large Language Models (LLMs) by shortening contexts, existing methods that specify a target compression ratio or length suffer from unpredictable performance degradation, hindering their reliable deployment. We introduce a paradigm shift to Performance-oriented Context Compression (PoC), where developers specify an acceptable performance floor instead of a compression ratio. PoC employs a lightweight performance predictor to automatically find the most aggressive compression ratio that satisfies this constraint before steering an off-the-shelf compressor. We design and compare two predictor variants: a simple context-agnostic predictor and a more sophisticated context-aware one that considers the input's inherent compressibility. On both question-answering and summarization benchmarks, the context-aware predictor consistently achieves lower performance prediction error than the context-agnostic predictor, while the resulting context-aware PoC attains a superior overall performance. Our work paves the way for a more reliable, efficient, and performance-aware deployment of context compression for LLMs.
2603.19733.paper_summary_zh: 雖然上下文壓縮可以通過縮短上下文來減輕大型語言模型（LLMs）日益增長的推理成本，但現有的方法在指定目標壓縮比或長度時，會遭遇不可預測的性能下降，妨礙其可靠的部署。我們引入了一種以性能為導向的上下文壓縮（PoC）的範式轉變，開發者指定可接受的性能底線，而不是壓縮比。PoC 使用輕量級性能預測器自動尋找滿足此約束的最激進壓縮比，然後引導現成的壓縮器。我們設計並比較了兩種預測器變體：一種是簡單的上下文無關預測器，另一種是更複雜的上下文感知預測器，考慮了輸入的固有可壓縮性。在問答和摘要基準測試中，上下文感知預測器始終實現了比上下文無關預測器更低的性能預測誤差，而最終的上下文感知 PoC 則達到了更優越的整體性能。我們的工作為大型語言模型的上下文壓縮提供了更可靠、高效和以性能為導向的部署途徑。
2603.19733.author: Runsong Zhao et.al.
2603.19733.authors: Runsong Zhao, Shilei Liu, Jiwei Tang, Langming Liu, Haibin Chen, Weidong Zhang, Yujin Yuan, Tong Xiao, Jingbo Zhu, Wenbo Su, Bo Zheng
